title: Living in Boulder vlog 5 - 13 - 2017: Athanasius & St Augustine are prolific

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs at 5am about spending the previous day cataloguing classical-world books for his personal website. He discusses the prolific output of early Christian writers like Athanasius and Augustine, muses on how losing theological factions had their books burned, and shares an ambitious vision for a hyperlinked library where readers can click any reference to jump directly to the cited passage in context.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: believes they represent the most prolific writing period of the Roman Empire
notes they were largely destroyed by the winning side, so not his problem to deal with
excited about building a system where every spoken or written reference links directly to the source passage in context, calling it a whole new level of communication
feels he is making amazing progress and is addicted to the project like a crackhead

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday, I was working on uploading books from the classical world to my website.

claims.subject: classical world
claims.claim: There are many figures from the classical world that people haven't heard of.

claims.subject: Arianism
claims.claim: Arianism was a significant movement at the beginning of Christianity, but most of their books were destroyed.

claims.subject: Origen
claims.claim: Origen's works were also largely destroyed.

claims.subject: Athanasius
claims.claim: Athanasius is a prolific figure whose works survived because he was on the winning side of history.

claims.subject: Roman Empire
claims.claim: The most prolific time period of the Roman Empire was at the end, with all the Christian figures.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was listening to my computer read Roman laws and other historical texts to me.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm learning a lot but feel too high to talk much about it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm tired right now, but my hair looks good.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm addicted to working on my website and uploading books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm making rapid progress on uploading books to my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might extend my deadline for completing the project by another week.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to make a video about selling my van next Monday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to make all my street books available for download on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I hope to eventually figure out how to make this system work for other people's websites.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Having all historical books on my website will help me reference them easily in discussions.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to transcribe my vlogs and link references directly to the books on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This system would revolutionize communication by allowing direct links to specific references.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm motivated by the potential of this system to impress historical figures like early Christians.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I already have a link on my homepage about the distribution of the coaching industry.

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder, V-Log. It's May 14th. Right now, 5.10 in the morning. So I'll talk about yesterday. I'm kind of having a little bit of an asthma attack right now.

So, yeah, I got up kind of late yesterday. It was like 2 in the afternoon or something because I was working until sunrise the day before. And so I was just working all through the day and the night. But I'm going to get to sleep now. I was thinking about going to the store to get candy though first, kind of have the munchies. But then that might keep me awake.

But I love sleeping in the morning because it's so peaceful to just sleep there during the day. So yesterday, I'm still working on putting the books from the classical world on my website. And there's lots of them. You know, as you dig around, you start to realize that there's lots. There's dudes you haven't heard of.
But luckily for me, I don't have to do all of them because most of them are destroyed. Like there's this whole thing, you know, Arianism.
You're in the beginning of Christianity, but like he lost. So they burned all his books. And I think the same thing with origin and stuff. So only the winning side, you have to copy their material.
But those guys have a lot. Like Athanasius. I didn't really know who that guy was, but he's got way a lot.
It's like St. Augustine. So I've been doing him. And then I did some St. Augustine because he's like the most prolific out of all of them.
So, yeah, the most, you know, I'll know when I'm done. And it'll be interesting to find out.
But I think the most prolific time period of the Roman Empire is right there at the end with all those Christians going at it.
So that's interesting. Someone listen to it, figure it out, do videos on it and stuff.
So that's what I was. And then I was listening to, you know, with my computer reading to me, Roman laws and stuff having to do with different Roman laws.
And then bits and pieces of the books that I'm listening to, the introductions and stuff.
So, yeah, I'm learning a lot. I'm learning a lot. I'm too high to be able to talk that much about it, though.
But I think I did talk some just now for two minutes and 47 seconds. That's pretty good.
I'm pretty high right now. I'm pretty high, pretty tired.
But my hair is looking good. So, yeah, so that's really all I have to say.
I'm totally addicted to it, like a crackhead, like a heroin, because, like, I haven't, you know, I haven't been working on this, on my website on this fast computer for a very long time.
And I was doing it on cell computers for so long. I don't know what I was thinking, but let's put that all behind me.
I am going at an amazing pace, though. Like, when I say I'm going to get every book on my website, I ain't joking.
I might go another week, though, you know, if I don't get it by this Sunday, I'll just give myself another week.
So I'll do my next Sunday. But on Monday, I definitely will do the video of my van, try to sell my van.
Just that my mom hasn't been putting the pressure on me, you know what I mean?
So I'm just, like, back on the sideline, like, oh, my God, what the fuck can I do?
Because, oh, yeah, let me tell you, this is what I want to talk about.
See, and I'm going to make it for anybody to be able to download all of my history books and put them on their websites.
They can do the same thing, because it only works if you do it with your own website.
Hopefully, maybe one day they'll figure out a way to do it for other people's websites.
But see, if you're talking about, like, you know, arguing somebody, some kind of philosophical or religious, historical, whatever,
If you have every single history book on your website and you've read through all of them and you pretty much have them memorized, you know,
If you go through them enough, you basically, you remember the word, you remember the book,
You remember the word and the general area, you can find it, you know.
You know, do control left, because it's all in one book.
And so then, whenever you're writing something, you know, and in the future, I want to, you can transcribe whatever you say,
Like your V-logs, because you just speak clearly and you have your computer do it.
I want to figure out how to have my computer do it.
And then you just put that on your website.
Maybe I should try it now on my V-logs, that'd be interesting.
But, and then you can, whatever you say or reference, you can reference literally to that spot, you know,
And you can be like, St. Augustine says in here, you know, this book,
But you just click it and it actually takes you to that spot in the book, in the context.
That is way, that's like taking it up a whole new, to a whole new level of communication
That would put, that would make those, those guys who are arguing back in the beginning of Christianity,
Very amazed with, with what we can do, envious of us.
So, that's why I'm motivated.
But yeah, I can't wait to listen through these and actually do it into this one.
But I already have it one link on my homepage.
You can link to the Levy, where he talks about the distribution of wealth causing,
Good distribution of wealth causing a healthy country.
Okay, anyway, that's enough speaking.
Peace out.
